str.signature: class str(object='') / class str(object=b'', encoding='utf-8', errors='strict')
str.description: Immutable sequence of Unicode code points. The standard text sequence type in Python.

format.description: Perform a string formatting operation. The string on which this method is called can contain literal text or replacement fields delimited by braces {}.

join.description: Return a string which is the concatenation of the strings in iterable, separated by the string providing this method.

split.description: Return a list of the words in the string, using sep as the delimiter string. If sep is None, splits on whitespace.

rsplit.description: Return a list of the words in the string, splitting from the right.

strip.description: Return a copy with leading and trailing characters removed.

lstrip.description: Return a copy with leading characters removed.

rstrip.description: Return a copy with trailing characters removed.

replace.description: Return a copy with all occurrences of substring old replaced by new. If count is given, only the first count occurrences are replaced.

find.description: Return the lowest index where substring sub is found within the slice s[start:end]. Return -1 if not found.

rfind.description: Return the highest index where substring sub is found. Return -1 if not found.

index.description: Like find(), but raise ValueError when the substring is not found.

count.description: Return the number of non-overlapping occurrences of substring sub.

startswith.description: Return True if string starts with the prefix, otherwise return False. prefix can be a tuple of prefixes to look for.

endswith.description: Return True if the string ends with the specified suffix, otherwise return False. suffix can be a tuple.

upper.description: Return a copy with all cased characters converted to uppercase.

lower.description: Return a copy with all cased characters converted to lowercase.

title.description: Return a titlecased version of the string where words start with an upper case character.

capitalize.description: Return a copy with its first character capitalized and the rest lowercased.

casefold.description: Return a casefolded copy of the string. Used for caseless matching.

swapcase.description: Return a copy with uppercase characters converted to lowercase and vice versa.

encode.description: Return the string encoded to bytes using the specified encoding.

isdigit.description: Return True if all characters in the string are digits.

isalpha.description: Return True if all characters in the string are alphabetic.

isalnum.description: Return True if all characters in the string are alphanumeric.

isspace.description: Return True if all characters are whitespace.

isupper.description: Return True if all cased characters are uppercase.

islower.description: Return True if all cased characters are lowercase.

zfill.description: Pad a numeric string on the left with zeros to fill a field of the given width.

center.description: Return centered in a string of the given width, padded with fillchar.

ljust.description: Return left-justified in a string of the given width.

rjust.description: Return right-justified in a string of the given width.

partition.description: Split the string at the first occurrence of sep. Returns (before, sep, after) or (str, '', '') if sep not found.

rpartition.description: Split the string at the last occurrence of sep.

removeprefix.description: If the string starts with prefix, return string[len(prefix):]. Otherwise return the original string. (3.9+)

removesuffix.description: If the string ends with suffix, return string[:-len(suffix)]. Otherwise return the original string. (3.9+)

list.description: Mutable sequence type. Lists may be constructed with square brackets, the list() constructor, or list comprehensions.

append.description: Append object to the end of the list.

extend.description: Extend list by appending elements from the iterable.

remove.description: Remove first occurrence of value. Raises ValueError if the value is not present.

pop.description: Remove and return item at index (default last). Raises IndexError if list is empty or index is out of range.

clear.description: Remove all items from the list.

index.description: Return first index of value. Raises ValueError if the value is not present.

count.description: Return number of occurrences of value.

sort.description: Sort the list in ascending order and return None. The sort is stable. key specifies a function of one argument used to extract a comparison key.

reverse.description: Reverse the elements of the list in place.

copy.description: Return a shallow copy of the list.

dict.signature: class dict(**kwargs) / class dict(mapping, **kwargs) / class dict(iterable, **kwargs)
dict.description: Mutable mapping type. Dictionaries map hashable keys to arbitrary values. Preserves insertion order (guaranteed since 3.7).

get.description: Return the value for key if key is in the dictionary, else default.

keys.description: Return a view object of the dictionary's keys.

values.description: Return a view object of the dictionary's values.

items.description: Return a view object of the dictionary's (key, value) pairs.

update.description: Update the dictionary with key/value pairs from other dict or iterable of key/value pairs.

pop.description: Remove specified key and return the corresponding value. If key is not found, default is returned if given, otherwise KeyError is raised.

popitem.description: Remove and return the last inserted (key, value) pair. Raises KeyError if the dict is empty.

setdefault.description: If key is in the dictionary, return its value. If not, insert key with a value of default and return default.

clear.description: Remove all items from the dictionary.

copy.description: Return a shallow copy of the dictionary.

fromkeys.description: Create a new dictionary with keys from iterable and values set to value.

set.description: Mutable unordered collection of unique hashable elements. Supports mathematical set operations: union, intersection, difference, symmetric difference.

add.description: Add element elem to the set.

remove.description: Remove element elem from the set. Raises KeyError if elem is not contained.

discard.description: Remove element elem from the set if it is present. Does not raise an error if absent.

pop.description: Remove and return an arbitrary element. Raises KeyError if the set is empty.

clear.description: Remove all elements from the set.

update.description: Update the set, adding elements from all others. Equivalent to s |= other.

union.description: Return a new set with elements from the set and all others. Equivalent to s | other.

intersection.description: Return a new set with elements common to the set and all others. Equivalent to s & other.

intersection_update.description: Update the set, keeping only elements found in it and all others. Equivalent to s &= other.

difference.description: Return a new set with elements in the set that are not in the others. Equivalent to s - other.

difference_update.description: Remove all elements of other sets from this set. Equivalent to s -= other.

symmetric_difference.description: Return a new set with elements in either the set or other but not both. Equivalent to s ^ other.

issubset.description: Test whether every element in the set is in other. Equivalent to s <= other.

issuperset.description: Test whether every element in other is in the set. Equivalent to s >= other.

isdisjoint.description: Return True if the set has no elements in common with other.

copy.description: Return a shallow copy of the set.

tuple.description: Immutable sequence type. Tuples are commonly used for heterogeneous data and as dictionary keys (since they are hashable). Support indexing, slicing, packing/unpacking.

count.description: Return number of occurrences of value.

index.description: Return first index of value. Raises ValueError if the value is not present.

int.signature: class int(x=0) / class int(x: str, base=10)
int.description: Immutable arbitrary-precision integer type. Supports standard arithmetic, comparison, bitwise operations, and conversions.

bit_length.description: Return the number of bits necessary to represent the integer in binary, excluding the sign and leading zeros.

bit_count.description: Return the number of ones in the binary representation (popcount). (3.10+)

to_bytes.description: Return an array of bytes representing the integer.

from_bytes.description: Return the integer represented by the given array of bytes.

conjugate.description: Returns self. Exists for compatibility with complex numbers.

real.description: The real part of the number (itself).

imag.description: The imaginary part of the number (always 0).

float.description: Immutable floating-point number implemented using C double (IEEE 754 double precision, 64-bit).

is_integer.description: Return True if the float instance is finite with integral value.

hex.description: Return a representation of a float as a hexadecimal string.

fromhex.description: Class method to return a float from a hexadecimal string.

as_integer_ratio.description: Return a pair of integers whose ratio is exactly equal to the float.

conjugate.description: Returns self. Exists for compatibility with complex numbers.

real.description: The real part of the number (itself).

imag.description: The imaginary part of the number (always 0.0).

bool.description: Subclass of int with exactly two instances: True (== 1) and False (== 0). Falsy values: None, 0, '', [], {}, set(), b''.

bytes.signature: class bytes(source=b'') / class bytes(source: str, encoding: str) / class bytes(length: int)
bytes.description: Immutable sequence of bytes (integers in range 0-255). Supports many str-like methods.

decode.description: Decode the bytes using the given encoding and return the resulting string.

hex.description: Return a string containing two hexadecimal digits per byte.

fromhex.description: Create a bytes object from a string of hexadecimal digits.

split.description: Return a list of the sections in the bytes, using sep as the delimiter.

join.description: Concatenate bytes objects from the iterable, separated by this bytes object.

replace.description: Return a copy with all occurrences of old replaced by new.

startswith.description: Return True if bytes starts with the prefix.

endswith.description: Return True if bytes ends with the suffix.

find.description: Return the lowest index where sub is found, or -1.

count.description: Return the number of non-overlapping occurrences of sub.

range.signature: class range(stop) / class range(start, stop, step=1)
range.description: Immutable sequence of numbers. Commonly used for looping a specific number of times in for loops. Memory efficient: only stores start, stop, step regardless of range size.

start.description: The value of the start parameter (0 if not provided).

stop.description: The value of the stop parameter.

step.description: The value of the step parameter (1 if not provided).

count.description: Return number of occurrences of value.

index.description: Return index of value. Raises ValueError if the value is not present.

frozenset.description: Immutable version of set. Being immutable and hashable, frozensets can be used as dictionary keys or as elements of other sets.

union.description: Return a new frozenset with elements from the frozenset and all others.

intersection.description: Return a new frozenset with elements common to the frozenset and all others.

difference.description: Return a new frozenset with elements in the frozenset that are not in the others.

symmetric_difference.description: Return a new frozenset with elements in either set but not both.

issubset.description: Test whether every element in the frozenset is in other.

issuperset.description: Test whether every element in other is in the frozenset.

isdisjoint.description: Return True if the frozenset has no elements in common with other.

copy.description: Return a shallow copy of the frozenset.

print.signature: print(*objects, sep=' ', end='\n', file=sys.stdout, flush=False) -> None
print.description: Print objects to the text stream file, separated by sep and followed by end.

parameters.name: sep
parameters.description: String inserted between values, default a space

parameters.name: end
parameters.description: String appended after the last value, default a newline

parameters.name: file
parameters.description: A file-like object (stream); defaults to sys.stdout

parameters.name: flush
parameters.description: Whether to forcibly flush the stream

len.description: Return the number of items in a container.

parameters.name: s
parameters.description: An object with a __len__ method (sequence, mapping, set, etc.)

enumerate.description: Return an enumerate object. Yields pairs containing a count (from start) and the values obtained from iterating over iterable.

zip.description: Iterate over several iterables in parallel, producing tuples with an item from each one. Stops when the shortest iterable is exhausted (unless strict=True).

parameters.name: *iterables
parameters.description: One or more iterables to zip together

parameters.name: strict
parameters.description: If True, raise ValueError if iterables have different lengths (3.10+)

map.description: Return an iterator that applies function to every item of iterable, yielding the results.

parameters.name: function
parameters.description: A function to apply to each element

parameters.name: iterable
parameters.description: Iterable(s) whose elements are passed to function

filter.description: Return an iterator yielding those items of iterable for which function(item) is true. If function is None, return items that are true.

parameters.name: function
parameters.description: A function that tests each element, or None for truth testing

sorted.description: Return a new sorted list from the items in iterable. The sort is guaranteed to be stable.

parameters.name: key
parameters.description: A function of one argument used to extract a comparison key

parameters.name: reverse
parameters.description: If True, sort in descending order

open.signature: open(file, mode='r', buffering=-1, encoding=None, errors=None, newline=None, closefd=True, opener=None) -> IO
open.description: Open file and return a corresponding file object. If the file cannot be opened, an OSError is raised. Should be used with a `with` statement for guaranteed cleanup.

parameters.name: file
parameters.description: Path to the file or a file descriptor

parameters.name: encoding
parameters.description: Text encoding (e.g. 'utf-8'). Only used in text mode

isinstance.description: Return True if the object argument is an instance of the classinfo argument, or of a (direct, indirect, or virtual) subclass thereof.

parameters.name: classinfo
parameters.description: A type, tuple of types, or union type to check against

type.signature: type(object) -> type / type(name, bases, dict, **kwds) -> type
type.description: With one argument, return the type of an object. With three arguments, create a new type object (dynamic class creation).

parameters.name: object
parameters.description: Object whose type to return (single-argument form)

parameters.name: dict
parameters.description: Namespace dict containing class body definitions (three-argument form)

super.description: Return a proxy object that delegates method calls to a parent or sibling class. The zero-argument form works inside a class definition.

parameters.name: object_or_type
parameters.description: Determines the MRO to search and the binding

abs.description: Return the absolute value of a number. The argument may be an integer, float, or object implementing __abs__().

min.signature: min(iterable, *, key=None, default=...) / min(arg1, arg2, *args, key=None)
min.description: Return the smallest item in an iterable or the smallest of two or more arguments.

parameters.name: default
parameters.description: Value to return if the iterable is empty

max.signature: max(iterable, *, key=None, default=...) / max(arg1, arg2, *args, key=None)
max.description: Return the largest item in an iterable or the largest of two or more arguments.

parameters.name: default
parameters.description: Value to return if the iterable is empty

sum.description: Sums start and the items of an iterable from left to right and returns the total.

round.description: Round a number to a given precision in decimal digits. Uses banker's rounding (round-half-to-even).

parameters.name: ndigits
parameters.description: Number of decimal digits. None returns an int.

input.description: Read a line from input, convert it to a string (stripping trailing newline), and return that.

parameters.name: prompt
parameters.description: The prompt string written to standard output

repr.description: Return a string containing a printable representation of an object. For many types, this returns a string that would yield an object with the same value when passed to eval().

hash.description: Return the hash value of the object (if it has one). Hash values are integers used to quickly compare dictionary keys during lookup.

id.description: Return the identity of an object — guaranteed unique integer for this object during its lifetime. In CPython, this is the memory address.

dir.description: Without arguments, return the list of names in the current local scope. With an argument, return a list of valid attributes for that object.

getattr.description: Return the value of the named attribute of object. If default is provided, it is returned if the attribute doesn't exist.

parameters.name: default
parameters.description: Value returned if attribute doesn't exist

setattr.description: Set the named attribute on the given object to the specified value.

hasattr.description: Return True if the string is the name of one of the object's attributes.

callable.description: Return True if the object appears callable (has a __call__ method).

iter.description: Return an iterator object. The first form expects an iterable. The second form calls callable until sentinel is returned.

parameters.name: object
parameters.description: An iterable, or a callable (sentinel form)

parameters.name: sentinel
parameters.description: Stop value for the callable form

next.description: Retrieve the next item from the iterator by calling __next__(). If the iterator is exhausted and default is given, return it instead of raising StopIteration.

parameters.name: default
parameters.description: Default value if iterator is exhausted

reversed.description: Return a reverse iterator over the values of the given sequence.

any.description: Return True if any element of the iterable is true. If the iterable is empty, return False.

all.description: Return True if all elements of the iterable are true (or if the iterable is empty).

breakpoint.description: Call sys.breakpointhook(), which by default drops into pdb. Set PYTHONBREAKPOINT=0 to disable.

pow.description: Return base to the power exp. If mod is present, return base**exp % mod (computed efficiently).

divmod.description: Return the pair (a // b, a % b).

format.description: Convert a value to a formatted representation as controlled by format_spec.

vars.description: Return the __dict__ attribute of the given object. Without arguments, acts like locals().

globals.description: Return the dictionary implementing the current module namespace.

locals.description: Return a mapping of the current local symbol table.

exec.description: Dynamically execute Python code. code can be a string or code object.

eval.description: Evaluate a single Python expression and return its value.

compile.signature: compile(source, filename, mode, flags=0, dont_inherit=False, optimize=-1) -> code
compile.description: Compile source into a code or AST object.

parameters.name: filename
parameters.description: Name of the file the code was read from

parameters.name: mode
parameters.description: 'exec' for module, 'eval' for expression, 'single' for interactive

issubclass.description: Return True if class is a subclass (direct, indirect, or virtual) of classinfo.

parameters.name: classinfo
parameters.description: A type or tuple of types

delattr.description: Delete the named attribute from the given object.

hex.description: Convert an integer to a lowercase hexadecimal string prefixed with '0x'.

oct.description: Convert an integer to an octal string prefixed with '0o'.

bin.description: Convert an integer to a binary string prefixed with '0b'.

ord.description: Return the Unicode code point for a one-character string.

chr.description: Return the string representing a character whose Unicode code point is the integer i.
